title: acer stachyophyllum subsp. betulifolium
intro: Acer stachyophyllum subsp. betulifolium, also known as the Betula-leaved Maple, is a subspecies of maple tree native to the mountains of Ecuador. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, derived from its bark, leaves, and roots. The health benefits of Acer stachyophyllum subsp. betulifolium include treating fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and cardiovascular protective properties. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, are made by extracting its active compounds to harness its medicinal properties.
